name: Trump
why_line: Trump tears up USMCA rather than renew it — the continental trade deal becomes a renegotiation cudgel aimed at Beijing's back door through Mexico.

name: Musk
why_line: SpaceX gets fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100, forcing every QQQ tracker to buy — Musk converts the largest IPO in history into automatic, index-mandated demand.

name: Altman
why_line: The Transformer's own co-inventor abandons Gemini for pre-IPO OpenAI — Altman raids Pichai's brain trust on the same day Amodei takes the Nobel man, a two-front talent bleed with DeepMind on the losing end of both.

name: Warsh
why_line: Warsh doubles down — 'prices are too high' and no more roadmap, the new chair telling the market to navigate a 4% world blind while he plants the hawkish flag deeper.

name: Amodei
why_line: DeepMind's hemorrhage becomes a rout — the Nobel laureate behind AlphaFold walks to Anthropic, and the story is Pichai's lab bleeding marquee science, not just Amodei's haul.

name: Abel
why_line: Berkshire's new hand: tripled the Alphabet stake then deployed $16.8B more - the quietest capital in America just picked a side in the AI war.

name: Dimon
why_line: Dimon clears the Fed stress test and immediately fires a $50B buyback while BofA flinches — the banker in every room now has regulatory permission to print.

name: Andreessen
why_line: a16z's capture machine gets a Pentagon badge — Andreessen wires himself into defense policy directly, turning the Valley-DC pipeline into a formal seat at the table.

name: Jassy
why_line: Jassy drops a billion on a Forward Deployed Engineering arm — Amazon copying the OpenAI-Anthropic enterprise playbook to force AWS into every boardroom deployment.

name: Bessent
why_line: Running Economic Fury, the sanctions campaign he's selling to the G7 - the administration's market-facing enforcer.

name: Nadella
why_line: Nadella takes the axe to sales, engineering, and Xbox — thousands out as Microsoft pays for its AI bet by gutting the legacy revenue centers that built it.

name: Warren
why_line: Lost the Warsh fight but owns the opposition - antagonist-in-chief to both other cities, and her bill is still moving.

name: Powell
why_line: Handed Warsh the chair but kept his Board seat - the in-house dissident with two years left and a warning shot already fired.

name: Bezos
why_line: Back in an operator's seat for the first time since Amazon - Prometheus at $41B with JPMorgan and BlackRock money, and Musk already calls him a copycat.

name: Fink
why_line: HPS put BlackRock's $14T machine deep inside the private credit stress - and his money is quietly inside Bezos's Prometheus too.

name: Hassabis
why_line: DeepMind's models power Gemini's run at ChatGPT's market share - the lab quietly winning the frontier race from inside Google.

name: Solomon
why_line: #1 in M&A with record revenues - Goldman's deal machine sits at the toll booth of the IPO wave Musk, Altman, and Amodei are about to ride.

name: Rowan
why_line: Gated his flagship credit fund at 45 cents on the requested dollar and called peers idiots - the loudest voice insisting the private credit fire is containable.

name: Atkins
why_line: The SEC's posture on crypto and the AI IPO wave runs through his desk - including whether Musk's consent order gets rolled back.

name: Ferguson
why_line: Antitrust is DC's main weapon against the Valley - the Google remedies fight and the platform probes run through his FTC.

name: Griffin
why_line: Feuding with New York's mayor, 'done sucking up' to the White House, backing a Spirit bailout - Wall Street's most openly political combatant.

name: Schwarzman
why_line: Blackstone loosened its gates while Apollo held firm - a public bet that confidence beats caution in the credit stress.

name: Huang
why_line: Etched books a billion in inference-chip orders at a $5B valuation — another crack in Huang's monopoly wall, coming days after OpenAI's Jalapeño told him the biggest buyers are building their own silicon.

name: Gray
why_line: The Senate moves 87-8 to wall institutional buyers out of single-family homes — a Washington supermajority aimed straight at the Blackstone playbook, and Gray's rental empire is the named target.

name: Karp
why_line: France's DGSI drops Palantir for a homegrown rival — the first allied spy agency to fire a US vendor in backlash to Washington's AI export crackdown, and Karp wears the first crack in the alliance.

name: Zuckerberg
why_line: Zuckerberg turns the AI capex bill into a revenue line — Meta will resell its excess compute, muscling straight into the cloud turf of Jassy, Pichai, and Nadella.

name: Pichai
why_line: DeepMind's hemorrhage becomes a rout — the Nobel laureate behind AlphaFold walks to Anthropic, and the story is Pichai's lab bleeding marquee science, not just Amodei's haul.

name: Blanche
why_line: Blanche's DOJ drops indictments on four shippers and seven executives — the acting AG showing teeth on enforcement while his own confirmation is still in motion.

name: Pulte
why_line: Acting DNI amid bipartisan fury - chaos generates events.

name: Krishnan
why_line: Departed WH AI advisor - watch where he lands.

name: Massie
why_line: Led the war-powers rebuke that cost the President 8 points.

name: Ellison
why_line: Oracle's AI spending blew past estimates and the credit market flinched - the debt bill on the buildout coming due in public.

name: Hegseth
why_line: Hegseth brags the Pentagon kicked Anthropic out 'forever' three months ago — and Mark Kelly's backing makes the pile-on bipartisan, the signal Amodei should fear most.

name: Thune
why_line: Thune strips Trump's voter-ID demand off the FISA bill and dares him to fight it — the Senate leader publicly choosing his own chamber over the President's wish list.

name: Cook
why_line: SCOTUS splits the baby and Trump still wins the war — Cook keeps her chair but the Court hands the President the FTC's neck, gutting the firewall that made independent agencies independent.
